renderer.c(3) Library Functions Manual renderer.c(3)

NAME

renderer.c - render_file_* — ties together font resolution, platform renderer, FFmpeg encoder

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct render_file_ctx_s

Functions


asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t **out)
void render_file_set_audio_source (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, void *audio_media_source, void *audio_capture_rb)
asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, const char *ansi_frame, uint64_t captured_ns)
void render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy (render_file_ctx_t *ctx)

Detailed Description

render_file_* — ties together font resolution, platform renderer, FFmpeg encoder

Definition in file renderer.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char * output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t ** out)

Definition at line 29 of file renderer.c.

30                                                               {
31   log_info("[RENDER_FILE_CREATE] CALLED with output_path=%s, cols=%d, rows=%d, fps=%d, theme=%d, out=%p",
32            output_path ? output_path : "(null)", cols, rows, fps, theme, (void *)out);
33   render_file_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*ctx), render_file_ctx_t *);
34 
35   // Resolve font spec → platform-appropriate file path, family name, or bundled data.
36   char font_spec[512] = {0};
37   bool font_is_path = false;
38   const uint8_t *font_data = NULL;
39   size_t font_data_size = 0;
40   const char *raw_font = GET_OPTION(render_font);
41 
42   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set, unless user explicitly overrides with --render-font
43   if ((!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
44     raw_font = "matrix";
45     log_debug("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Using matrix font (enabled by --matrix flag)");
46   }
47 
48   // BEFORE resolving fonts, check if matrix font and disable it for render-file (causes glyph height=0)
49   if (raw_font && strcmp(raw_font, "matrix") == 0) {
50     log_warn("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Matrix font not supported for render-file output, using system font instead");
51     raw_font = NULL;  // Force fallback to system font
52   }
53 
54   asciichat_error_t fe =
55       platform_font_resolve(raw_font, font_spec, sizeof(font_spec), &font_is_path, &font_data, &font_data_size);
56   if (fe != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
57     log_warn("renderer: font resolution failed for '%s' — using system default", raw_font ? raw_font : "(explicit system)");
58   }
59 
60   log_debug("render_file_create: Font resolved: font_spec='%s', font_is_path=%d, font_data=%p (size=%zu)", font_spec,
61             font_is_path, (void *)font_data, font_data_size);
62 
63   // Use reasonable default font size for render-file quality
64   double font_size_pt = GET_OPTION(render_font_size);
65 
66   term_renderer_config_t tr_cfg = {
67       .cols = cols,
68       .rows = rows,
69       .font_size_pt = font_size_pt,
70       .theme = (term_renderer_theme_t)theme,
71       .font_is_path = font_is_path,
72       .font_data = font_data,
73       .font_data_size = font_data_size,
74   };
75   strncpy(tr_cfg.font_spec, font_spec, sizeof(tr_cfg.font_spec) - 1);
76 
77   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_create(&tr_cfg, &ctx->renderer);
78   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
79     log_error("render_file_create: term_renderer_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
80     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
81     return err;
82   }
83   int actual_width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
84   int actual_height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
85   log_info("render_file_create: GRID DIMENSIONS: %ux%u cells -> PIXEL DIMENSIONS: %dx%d px", cols, rows,
86            actual_width_px, actual_height_px);
87   log_debug("render_file_create: term_renderer created (%dx%d cells, %dx%d px)", cols, rows, actual_width_px,
88             actual_height_px);
89 
90   err = ffmpeg_encoder_create(output_path, actual_width_px, actual_height_px, fps, &ctx->encoder);
91   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92     log_error("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
93     term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
94     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
95     return err;
96   }
97   log_debug("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder created successfully");
98 
99   // Initialize audio fields
100   ctx->audio_sample_rate = 48000;  // Audio pipeline is 48kHz
101   ctx->audio_buf_size = 1024;      // Temporary buffer for reading audio (in floats)
102   ctx->audio_read_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->audio_buf_size * sizeof(float), float *);
103   ctx->audio_media_source = NULL;
104   ctx->audio_capture_rb = NULL;
105 
106   log_info("renderer: initialized encoder for %s", output_path);
107 
108   // Register render file context for debugging
109   NAMED_REGISTER_CONTEXT(ctx, "render_file_ctx", output_path, NULL);
110 
111   *out = ctx;
112   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
113 }




References ffmpeg_encoder_create(), SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, term_renderer_create(),
    term_renderer_destroy(), term_renderer_height_px(), and
    term_renderer_width_px().

asciichat_error_t
  render_file_destroy (render_file_ctx_t * ctx)


Definition at line 276 of file renderer.c.


276                                                               {
277   if (!ctx)
278     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
279   asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
280   term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
281   SAFE_FREE(ctx->audio_read_buf);
282   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
283   return err;
284 }






References ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(), SAFE_FREE, and
    term_renderer_destroy().

115                                                                                                             {

117   ctx->audio_media_source = (media_source_t *)audio_media_source;
118   ctx->audio_capture_rb = (audio_ring_buffer_t *)audio_capture_rb;

asciichat_error_t
  render_file_write_frame (render_file_ctx_t * ctx, const char * ansi_frame,
  uint64_t captured_ns)


Definition at line 122 of file renderer.c.


122                                                                                                                 {
123   // Write to debug file directly to bypass logging system
124   FILE *dbg = fopen("/tmp/render-debug.txt", "a");
125   if (dbg) {
126     fprintf(dbg, "[RENDER_WRITE_FRAME] Called with ctx=%p, frame_len=%zu, captured_ns=%llu\n", (void *)ctx,
127             ansi_frame ? strlen(ansi_frame) : 0, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
128     fclose(dbg);
129   }
130   log_info("render_file_write_frame: CALLED - ctx=%p, captured_ns=%llu", (void *)ctx, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
131 
132   if (!ctx) {
133     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ctx is NULL");
134     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
135   }
136 
137   if (!ansi_frame) {
138     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ansi_frame is NULL");
139     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
140   }
141 
142   size_t frame_len = strlen(ansi_frame);
143   log_info("render_file_write_frame: processing frame (len=%zu)", frame_len);
144   if (frame_len > 0) {
145     log_info("  first 100 chars: %.100s", ansi_frame);
146   }
147 
148   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_feed(ctx->renderer, ansi_frame, frame_len);
149   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
150     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: term_renderer_feed failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
151     return err;
152   }
153 
154   const uint8_t *pixels = term_renderer_pixels(ctx->renderer);
155   int pitch = term_renderer_pitch(ctx->renderer);
156   int width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
157   int height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
158 
159   log_info("render_file_write_frame: pixels=%p pitch=%d dims=%dx%d", (void *)pixels, pitch, width_px, height_px);
160 
161   // Check pixels in multiple locations to verify content
162   if (pixels) {
163     // Check pixel at (0,0) - top left corner
164     uint8_t sample_r = pixels[0], sample_g = pixels[1], sample_b = pixels[2];
165 
166     // Check pixel at (500, 100) where we injected a test red stripe
167     size_t test_offset = 100 * pitch + 500 * 3;
168     uint8_t test_r = pixels[test_offset], test_g = pixels[test_offset + 1], test_b = pixels[test_offset + 2];
169 
170     // Check pixel at middle of screen
171     uint8_t sample_r_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch], sample_g_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 1],
172             sample_b_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 2];
173 
174     log_info("  pixel[0,0]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), test_stripe[500,100]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), pixel[0,%d]: RGB(%u,%u,%u)",
175              sample_r, sample_g, sample_b, test_r, test_g, test_b, height_px/2, sample_r_mid, sample_g_mid, sample_b_mid);
176   }
177 
178   // CRITICAL: Copy pixel buffer before encoding
179   // term_renderer_pixels() returns a pointer to the renderer's internal buffer which gets
180   // overwritten on the next frame. We must copy the data before passing to the encoder.
181   uint8_t *pixels_copy = NULL;
182   if (pixels) {
183     size_t pixel_buffer_size = (size_t)pitch * (size_t)height_px;
184     pixels_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(pixel_buffer_size, uint8_t *);
185     memcpy(pixels_copy, pixels, pixel_buffer_size);
186     log_info("render_file_write_frame: copied %zu bytes of pixel data (was at %p, now at %p)",
187              pixel_buffer_size, (void *)pixels, (void *)pixels_copy);
188 
189     // DEBUG: Inject test red stripe to verify encoder is working
190     static int frame_num = 0;
191     if (frame_num++ < 1) {
192       log_info("DEBUG: Injecting test RED vertical stripe into pixel buffer for verification");
193       // Fill a 100-pixel-wide vertical stripe with bright red at x=500
194       for (int y = 0; y < height_px; y++) {
195         for (int x = 500; x < 600 && x < width_px; x++) {
196           uint8_t *pixel = pixels_copy + y * pitch + x * 3;
197           pixel[0] = 255;  // R
198           pixel[1] = 0;    // G
199           pixel[2] = 0;    // B
200         }
201       }
202     }
203   }
204 
205   err = ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(ctx->encoder, pixels_copy, pitch, captured_ns);
206   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
207     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
208   }
209 
210   // Free the pixel copy (ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame reads and converts it immediately)
211   SAFE_FREE(pixels_copy);
212 
213   // Write synchronized audio for this frame if available
214   // Calculate how many samples correspond to one video frame
215   // Note: Audio samples per frame = 48000 Hz / FPS  (e.g., 1600 samples @ 30fps)
216   if (ctx && ctx->audio_read_buf && ctx->encoder) {
217     // Assume default 60 FPS if we can't determine it (typically matches capture rate)
218     int fps = 60;
219     int samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_sample_rate / fps;
220     if (samples_per_frame > ctx->audio_buf_size) {
221       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_buf_size;
222     }
223 
224     int samples_read = 0;
225     static int audio_debug_once = 0;
226 
227     // Read from whichever audio source is available (prefer media source, fallback to capture)
228     if (ctx->audio_media_source) {
229       // Read from media source (file/URL audio)
230       samples_read = media_source_read_audio(ctx->audio_media_source, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
231       if (samples_read < 0) {
232         samples_read = 0;  // On error, write silence
233       }
234       if (!audio_debug_once++) {
235         log_debug("render_file_write_frame: reading audio from media_source (samples_per_frame=%d, read=%d)",
236                   samples_per_frame, samples_read);
237       }
238     } else if (ctx->audio_capture_rb) {
239       // Read from ring buffer (live mic capture)
240       samples_read =
241           audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->audio_capture_rb, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
242       if (samples_read < 0) {
243         samples_read = 0;  // On error, write silence
244       }
245       if (!audio_debug_once++) {
246         log_debug("render_file_write_frame: reading audio from capture_rb (samples_per_frame=%d, read=%d)",
247                   samples_per_frame, samples_read);
248       }
249     } else {
250       if (!audio_debug_once++) {
251         log_debug("render_file_write_frame: no audio source available (media=%p, capture=%p)",
252                   ctx->audio_media_source, ctx->audio_capture_rb);
253       }
254     }
255 
256     // Write audio samples if any were read
257     if (samples_read > 0) {
258       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_read);
259     } else if (samples_read == 0 && (ctx->audio_media_source || ctx->audio_capture_rb)) {
260       // Source exists but no samples available - still need to write something for sync
261       // Zero-fill the buffer (silence) to maintain timing
262       memset(ctx->audio_read_buf, 0, samples_per_frame * sizeof(float));
263       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
264     }
265   }
266 
267   return err;
268 }






References audio_ring_buffer_read(),
    ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(), ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(),
    media_source_read_audio(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    term_renderer_feed(), term_renderer_height_px(),
    term_renderer_pitch(), term_renderer_pixels(), and
    term_renderer_width_px().


Referenced by session_display_encode_frame().
